data.title: UK and France to Convene Meeting on Hormuz Strait Shipping Security
data.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on the 13th that he will co-host a meeting with French President Emmanuel Macron this week to discuss shipping security in the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, Starmer addressed the UK Parliament, stating that the meeting will focus on diplomatic efforts to end conflicts, reopen the strait, and ensure safety for international shipping once stability is restored. Starmer emphasized that the UK's stance on the Middle East should prioritize de-escalation and diplomatic resolution of disputes. The UK will neither support nor participate in any blockade actions concerning the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: Crypto markets rallied across the board, with the DeFi sector leading the gains at 5%, and BTC breaking through $74,000.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, rising expectations of a US-Iran agreement have restored market confidence, leading to widespread gains in the crypto market, with the DeFi sector performing particularly well, up 5.00% in the last 24 hours. Hyperliquid (HYPE) rose 7.06%, while Lido DAO (LDO) and Aave (AAVE) rose 9.94% and 10.75% respectively. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 4.51%, surpassing $74,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 7.56%, surpassing $2,300. Notably, MAG7.ssi rose 4.23%, DEFI.ssi rose 6.22%, and MEME.ssi rose 3.27%. In other sectors, the RWA sector rose 4.05% in the last 24 hours, with Plume (PLUME) surging 13.92%; the CeFi sector rose 2.78%, and NEXO (NEXO) rose 3.79%; the Layer 1 sector rose 2.76%, and Algorand (ALGO) rose 8.11%; the Layer 2 sector rose 2.75%, and Optimism (OP) rose 6.90%; the PayFi sector rose 2.59%, and Telcoin (TEL) rose 12.07%; the Meme sector rose 1.85%, and Binance Life rose 13.20%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiDeFi, ssiLayer1, and ssiRWA indices rose 5.69%, 5.67%, and 4.65%, respectively.

data.title: Barclays: Thailand's Central Bank to Maintain Policy Rate at 1.00% Until 2026
data.description: On April 14, Jin10 reported that economists at Barclays have released a report indicating that the Bank of Thailand is expected to keep its policy rate at 1.00% until 2026. According to Jin10, the Governor of the Bank of Thailand recently stated that the central bank intends to maintain the current policy rate for as long as possible and avoid raising interest rates. Barclays highlighted that the central bank is more concerned about the impact of Middle Eastern conflicts on Thailand's economic growth rather than higher inflation rates. The report also noted that the Bank of Thailand has previously expressed a preference to overlook supply shocks and the subsequent rise in inflation.

data.title: LD Capital Founder Warns Against Altcoin Futures Trading
data.description: Jack Yi, founder of LD Capital, has expressed concerns over altcoin futures trading, describing it as a 'guaranteed losing game.' According to NS3.AI, Yi highlighted the inherent high-risk nature of altcoins, which makes futures trading particularly perilous. He noted a recent trend of traders facing liquidations in altcoin futures, underscoring the volatility and risk associated with these assets.

data.title: High-Leverage ETH Short Position Faces Multiple Liquidations
data.description: A cryptocurrency address has experienced eight liquidations within a few hours, leaving only $10,600 in the account. According to Foresight News, the address continues to maintain a high-leverage short position on Ethereum (ETH), with a new liquidation price set at $2,399.37.

data.title: NFT buyers who support community members' medical expenses will receive Rave DAO airdrops, with returns ranging from 20x to 500x.
data.description: Crypto KOL LEO posted on the X platform that last December, @ufukless1 needed to pay medical expenses and issued a $10 NFT on OpenSea, with all 15,000 NFTs minted. Subsequently, Rave DAO airdropped RAVE tokens to all minters. This airdrop is currently worth between $200 and $5,000, representing a return of at least 20 times.

data.title: Britain and France will convene a meeting on shipping safety in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on the 13th that he will co-host a leaders' meeting this week with French President Emmanuel Macron on shipping security in the Strait of Hormuz. Starmer told the British Parliament that the meeting will focus on ending the conflict through diplomatic means, reopening the Strait, and providing security guarantees for international shipping once the situation stabilizes. Starmer emphasized that Britain's position on the Middle East situation should prioritize de-escalation and promoting a diplomatic solution to the dispute. Britain will neither support nor participate in any blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iranian Ambassador to India: Indian oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz have not paid any passage fees to Iran.
data.description: Iran's ambassador to India said on Monday that Indian oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz have not paid any tolls to Iran. This comes after US President Trump vowed to prevent ships that pay fees to Iran from passing through the strait. "You can ask the Indian government whether we have collected any fees so far," Iranian Ambassador to India Mohammad Fatahari said at a press conference at the Iranian embassy. "We maintain good relations during this difficult time. We believe that Iran and India share common interests and a common destiny," he added. Iran has effectively blocked the strategic waterway since the outbreak of the Iran-Iraq War. India has repeatedly denied paying any fees. India says 15 Indian-flagged vessels remain stranded in the Persian Gulf. (Jinshi)

data.title: Lighter Launches Liquidity Partnership Program for RWA
data.description: Decentralized perpetual contract platform Lighter has introduced a liquidity partnership program to reward participants providing liquidity for Real World Assets (RWA). According to Foresight News, the program will offer incentives to market makers who supply compact and deep liquidity on Lighter's order book. Rewards are distributed through random snapshots of the order book, with the amount to be awarded announced in advance each week. Participants must hold a valid Premium account, and only orders present on the order book at the time of each snapshot are eligible. Assets are categorized into different tiers based on their liquidity characteristics to manage their relative share in the reward pool.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Low Probability of Israeli Strike on Yemen
data.description:
The probability of Israel launching a strike on Yemen by April 30 has decreased to 14%, according to Odaily. This marks a 32% drop in the past 24 hours on the Polymarket platform.

U.S. Secretary of State Rubio is set to host talks between Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors in Washington on the 14th. These discussions come amid ongoing conflicts, focusing on the possibility of a ceasefire, Hezbollah's disarmament, and a long-term peace agreement. Following pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump to de-escalate tensions, Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has agreed to advance negotiations. The U.S. has requested Israel to pause certain military actions.

As diplomatic efforts commence, expectations for Israel to open a new front in Yemen have significantly diminished.

data.title: Global Oil Market Faces Restructuring Amid U.S.-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Since the outbreak of the U.S.-Iran conflict on February 28, the global oil market is undergoing a significant restructuring of power dynamics. According to BlockBeats, on April 2, the price of WTI crude oil for near-month contracts surpassed Brent crude for the first time in nearly four years, highlighting the harsh reality of energy supply chain reconfiguration during wartime.

The core logic behind this price inversion lies in the repricing of 'physical security.' Traditionally, Brent crude has enjoyed a premium due to its representation of global maritime trade flows. However, with the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, Brent-related crude from the Persian Gulf, Oman, and the UAE now carries a 'risk discount,' leading to soaring tanker insurance premiums and a complete halt in some shipments. In contrast, WTI crude, transported directly to Gulf Coast refineries via a mature pipeline network, benefits from 'land route advantages,' becoming a key competitive edge in this crisis.

Germini Energy founder Germini noted, "The market reacts swiftly—buyers are now paying premiums for oil they can physically obtain rather than for oil representing the global market."

From a market structure perspective, an extreme 'spot premium' pattern has emerged. Currently, the December delivery price for WTI contracts is around $77 per barrel, approximately $25 lower than the May contracts. Investors are frantically purchasing spot oil to address current supply disruptions while betting on a potential easing of the conflict in the coming months. In the physical spot market, some Brent crude prices have exceeded $140 per barrel.

Stratas Advisors President Percy warned that with the U.S. announcing a naval blockade on Iranian ports, the premium situation is becoming more complex, with spot Brent prices potentially testing the $160 to $190 range in the coming weeks. If prices remain high for an extended period, it could trigger severe 'demand destruction,' forcing consumers to significantly reduce usage and potentially leading to a global economic recession. Analysts suggest this might be the only leverage to bring both the U.S. and Iran back to the negotiating table.

data.title: Iran's Uranium Compromise Signals Potential End to Conflict
data.description:
Liquid Capital founder JackYi expressed optimism on social media about the potential end of the ongoing conflict, as Iran shows willingness to abandon uranium enrichment as a compromise. According to Foresight News, JackYi hopes that world peace will not remain just a slogan, and anticipates a rebound in the market unless hostilities resume.

He also commented on the risks associated with trading altcoin contracts, noting that such investments are inherently high-risk. JackYi emphasized that combining altcoins with contracts is a losing strategy, especially when facing opponents with control over capital and technology. He referenced Warren Buffett's advice that gradual wealth accumulation is the best approach, warning that even after five consecutive wins, a single loss can wipe out gains, which is not a sustainable business model.

data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Israel launching an attack on Yemen before April 30th has fallen to 14% on Polymarket, a 32% drop over the past 24 hours. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will host a meeting between the Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors in Washington on the 14th. This meeting, held amidst ongoing conflict, will focus on the possibility of a ceasefire, Hezbollah's disarmament, and a long-term peace agreement. It is understood that after President Trump pressured for de-escalation, Netanyahu agreed to move forward with negotiations, and the US has requested Israel to suspend some military operations. With diplomatic efforts underway, market expectations for Israel opening a new front in Yemen have cooled significantly. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before they are priced in.

data.title: Russian Authorities Arrest Telegram Bloggers for Stock Manipulation
data.description:
Russian authorities have arrested three bloggers from a Telegram investment channel, accusing them of manipulating the stocks of 19 companies on the Moscow Exchange through a 'pump and dump' strategy. According to Foresight News, the Federal Security Service (FSB) reported that the illegal activities involved over 55,000 transactions.

The Central Bank of Russia has noted a rise in cases where financial bloggers exploit their followers for profit, pledging to continue cracking down on such market manipulation practices. Research from Moscow University indicates that approximately 11% of investment signals on Telegram can directly influence stock price movements. Additionally, over 60% of retail investors in the Russian market heavily rely on social media signals for trading decisions.

data.title: Strategy bought $1 billion worth of BTC at $71,902, bringing its total BTC holdings to $57.83 billion.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, Saylor bought $1 billion worth of BTC at a price of $71,902. Strategy currently holds $57.83 billion worth of BTC, with an average purchase price of $75,577. These recent purchases were entirely funded by the sale of STRC.

data.title: Crypto stocks closed higher across the board in the US market, with Circle (CRCL) rising more than 12%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.63%, the S&P 500 rose 1.02%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 1.23%. The crypto sector collectively strengthened, with Circle rising 12.09%, Solana rising 7.41%, Coinbase rising 3.98%, Strategy (MSTR) rising 2.89%, SharpLink rising 2.76%, and Bitmine rising 1.08%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: A whale deposited $2.4 million USDC into HyperLiquid and bought HYPE at a price of $44.30.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $2.4 million USDC into HyperLiquid and bought 54,157 HYPE tokens at a price of $44.3.

data.title: Saudi Arabia urges the United States to lift the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Saudi Arabia is pressuring the United States to lift its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and return to the negotiating table. Saudi Arabia fears that President Trump's move to block the strait could lead to retaliatory action from Iran, potentially resulting in the closure of the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. The Bab el-Mandeb Strait is a key passage in the Red Sea and crucial for Saudi Arabia's approximately 7 million barrels of oil exports per day. Previously, Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz disrupted approximately 13 million barrels of oil exports per day, pushing oil prices above $100. Trump imposed a blockade on Iranian ports on Monday. Arab officials say Iran is pressuring the Houthi rebels in Yemen to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. Saudi energy officials say Saudi Arabia had previously received assurances from the Houthis that they would not attack its ships, but the situation remains uncertain. (WSJ)
